title: Stocks & Stuffs Demo Opportunity Snapshot

marketRegime.label: neutral
marketRegime.summary: Demo regime is mixed: large-cap trend remains constructive, but options and crypto signals require confirmation.
evidence: Major indexes above long-term trend in demo data

opportunities.name: NVIDIA Corp.
opportunities.thesis: Watch for continuation only if price reclaims short-term momentum with volume confirmation.
opportunities.explanation: The demo setup combines relative strength leadership with a pullback into a defined risk zone; patience is required to avoid chasing.
opportunities.catalyst: AI/semiconductor demand narrative and upcoming sector earnings read-throughs.
opportunities.technicalContext: Above rising 20/50 day moving averages in demo candle set; volume trend supportive.
opportunities.actionPlan: Candidate only on reclaim of entry zone with broad market support; avoid if indexes fade.

risk.positionSizingNote: Keep size modest; stop distance is wide for a short-term trade.

alerts: Use limit orders; demo quote may be stale.

opportunities.name: Microsoft Corp.
opportunities.thesis: Long-term watch candidate if valuation cools while cloud and AI fundamentals remain intact.
opportunities.explanation: The mock long-term setup prioritizes business quality and durable cash flow over near-term price momentum.
opportunities.catalyst: Cloud growth, AI platform monetization, and enterprise software resilience.
opportunities.technicalContext: Sideways consolidation in demo data; not an urgent breakout setup.
opportunities.actionPlan: Scale only near planned zones; keep dry powder for deeper pullbacks.

risk.positionSizingNote: Use long-term allocation sizing, not short-term leverage.

opportunities.name: SPY defined-risk call spread
opportunities.thesis: A defined-risk spread can express upside view while capping loss, but only after live option quotes are confirmed.
opportunities.explanation: This is a screening example, not an execution alert. Delayed options data and spread width require broker confirmation.
opportunities.catalyst: Potential broad-market continuation if macro data supports risk appetite.
opportunities.technicalContext: Index trend is neutral-to-up in demo data; setup needs confirmation above resistance.
opportunities.actionPlan: Watch-only until live chain confirms tight spreads, sufficient volume, and acceptable max loss.

risk.positionSizingNote: Defined-risk only; max loss must be acceptable before entry.
whatChangesMind: Expiration is too close for thesis

warnings: Options data is indicative/delayed and is not suitable for precise fills.
Confirm bid/ask and open interest with a licensed broker before acting.

opportunities.name: Bitcoin
opportunities.thesis: Watch for breakout confirmation rather than predicting direction inside a choppy range.
opportunities.explanation: The demo crypto card emphasizes volatility controls and confirmation because crypto can gap outside equity market hours.
opportunities.catalyst: ETF flows, macro risk appetite, and liquidity conditions.
opportunities.technicalContext: Range-bound in demo data with breakout trigger above resistance.
opportunities.actionPlan: Watch for confirmed close above range; avoid leverage in choppy conditions.

risk.positionSizingNote: Small sizing due to high volatility; no hidden leverage.

warnings: Crypto liquidity and volatility can change quickly.

alerts: Crypto data is aggregated and demo-only.

avoidList.name: Tesla Inc.
avoidList.thesis: Avoid chasing after a sharp move without a nearby invalidation level.
avoidList.explanation: The avoid-list example shows how the dashboard can recommend patience when risk is not well-defined.
avoidList.catalyst: Headline-driven volatility without a clean entry plan.
avoidList.technicalContext: Extended from moving averages in demo data; pullback risk elevated.
avoidList.actionPlan: Wait for a base, pullback, or defined invalidation before reconsidering.

risk.positionSizingNote: No position suggested; setup lacks favorable risk/reward.

alerts: Low-confidence ideas default away from action.
